author: codex-agent

patch.value: Fix Grafana RabbitMQ queue panel metric selector mismatch

Telemetry overview dashboard queue panels query rabbitmq_queue_messages{queue="pm.telemetry.events"} and rabbitmq_queue_messages_unacked{queue="pm.telemetry.events"}, but current Prometheus series on the remote stack have no queue label, so the panel query returns empty data despite non-zero queue depth.
Grafana queue panels render live queue depth and unacked values in production by aligning panel queries with actual metric labels (or by exposing the queue label in exporter config).

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm context --limit 10; pm search 'Grafana rabbitmq queue dashboard metric label' --limit 10; pm search 'queue label rabbitmq_queue_messages' --limit 10; pm list-open --limit 20; pm list-in-progress --limit 20. Net-new because no open item currently tracks dashboard query label mismatch between Prometheus RabbitMQ series and Grafana queue panel selectors.

message: Track Grafana queue panel metric mismatch from remote telemetry audit

value.note: Remote telemetry stack runbook used for panel diagnostics

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Remote evidence 2026-04-26: Prometheus query rabbitmq_queue_messages returns value 3 and rabbitmq_queue_messages_unacked returns value 3, but both series are exported without queue label in metric metadata. Dashboard expressions filtering queue=pm.telemetry.events therefore return empty vectors and hide active queue backlog.

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Fresh remote audit evidence 2026-04-26: Prometheus query rabbitmq_queue_messages{queue="pm.telemetry.events"} returned an empty vector, while unfiltered rabbitmq_queue_messages and rabbitmq_queue_messages_unacked returned non-zero values without a queue label. Grafana/API/Prometheus were healthy, so the dashboard panel issue remains a selector-label mismatch.
